LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITH ONE FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING ALLEGATIONS OF FAILURE TO PAY REGULAR WAGES (1ST CAUSE OF ACTION). PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING ALLEGATIONS OF FAILURE TO PROVIDE MEAL AND REST BREAK PERIODS (3RD CAUSE OF ACTION.) PLAINTIFF MUST COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 382 TO BRING A REPRESENTATIVE ACTION UNDER B&P SEC. 17200. ABSENT CLASS CERTIFICATION, RELIEF AND RESTITUTION CANNOT EXTEND BEYOND THE NAMED PLAINTIFF. HARPER VS. 24 HOUR FITNESS INC. 167 CAL.APP. 4TH 966. (5TH CAUSE OF ACTION). (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained, with one final leave to amend granted. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting allegations of failure to pay regular wages (1st cause of action). Plaintiff must allege facts supporting allegations of failure to provide meal and rest break periods (3rd cause of action.) Plaintiff must comply with CCP section 382 to bring a representative action under B&P sec. 17200. Absent class certification, relief and restitution cannot extend beyond the named plaintiff. Harper vs. 24 Hour Fitness Inc. 167 Cal.App. 4th 966. (5th cause of action). (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: Defendant Service Employees International Union Local 87 Motion for judgment on the pleadings (transferred from Department 301, to be heard with other Judgments on the pleadings). Appearances: Jason Lundberg, Esq. of Ladva Law Firm (415) 296-8844, appearing for Plaintiff; Dean Royer, Esq. & Jessica P. Albert, Esq. of Siegel & Yee (510) 839-1200 appearing for Defendant. (This tentative ruling as amended is not being contested.) The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Granted with thirty days leave to amend as to first, second, third, and fifth cause of action.
